[鲁]
Simplified from 魯 ( 魚 → 鱼 ).

[魯]
Originally ideogrammic compound ( 會意 / 会意 ): 魚 ( “ fish ” ) + 口 ( “ mouth ” ) — fish entering the mouth; hence "delicious" → "excellent". In oracle bone and bronze inscriptions, often written simply as 魚 ; 口 was added to differentiate this word from "fish". The 口 component later merged with the fish tail to resemble 白 , leading to the Shuowen misanalysis 从白，鮺省聲 .
